name: Melqart
description: Melqart was a major Phoenician deity, particularly venerated in Tyre, from which his worship spread throughout the Mediterranean. He was considered a protector of the city and its colonies, a god of navigation and the sea, and a symbol of royal power and prosperity. His name, likely meaning "King of the City" or "Lord of the City," reflects his role as a civic and royal deity. Melqart was often associated with Heracles (Hercules) by the Greeks, and the Roman Hercules was identified with him. He embodies strength, courage, and seafaring prowess. Temples dedicated to Melqart were often situated near harbors, reflecting his importance to maritime trade and travel. He was also connected to the underworld and cycles of death and rebirth, possibly linked to agricultural fertility. Melqart's festivals were celebrated with athletic competitions, processions, and sacrifices, showcasing his multifaceted nature as a god of both physical strength and civic well-being. He acted as a guardian figure, ensuring the safety and success of Tyrian ventures across the seas and beyond. The worship of Melqart played a significant role in Phoenician expansion and cultural influence throughout the ancient world, shaping religious landscapes from Spain to North Africa and beyond. Offerings to Melqart varied from incense and animal sacrifices to dedications of ships and other seafaring equipment, highlighting his importance to the maritime activities of the Phoenicians. His enduring legacy is evident in the numerous archaeological finds and historical accounts that attest to his widespread and profound influence.
subtitle: King of the City, Tyrian Protector

keyMyths.title: Melqart's Journey
keyMyths.description: Melqart is said to have traveled widely, establishing Tyrian colonies and spreading civilization. His journeys mirror the Phoenician expansion across the Mediterranean, solidifying his role as a culture hero.

keyMyths.title: Melqart and the Founding of Tyre
keyMyths.description: According to some accounts, Melqart was instrumental in the founding or early development of Tyre. He ensured the city's prosperity and protected it from enemies, establishing its prominence in the ancient world.

keyMyths.title: Melqart's Resurrection
keyMyths.description: Some interpretations suggest Melqart underwent a cycle of death and resurrection, aligning him with agricultural deities. This cycle symbolized renewal and the continuation of life, connecting him to the earth's fertility.

sources.author: Herodotus
sources.description: Mentions the Temple of Heracles at Tyre and its association with Melqart.
